ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "imdb_id" INTEGER /* IMDb identifier for the movie */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the movie was released */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Duration of the movie in minutes */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget of the movie in dollars */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated by the movie in dollars */,
  "tagline" TEXT /* Tagline or slogan of the movie */,
  "homepage" TEXT /* Official website of the movie */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the movie (e.g., 'Released', 'In Production') */,
  "production_companies" TEXT /* JSON array of production companies involved in the movie */,
  "production_countries" TEXT /* JSON array of countries where the movie was produced */,
  "spoken_languages" TEXT /* JSON array of languages spoken in the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the genre */,
  "parent_genre_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the parent genre, to support hierarchical genre structures */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the movie-genre relationship */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre */,
  "primary_genre" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the genre is the primary genre of the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_genres_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_genres_genre_id FOREIGN KEY ("genre_id") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie rated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who provided the rating */,
  "rating" REAL /* Rating value provided by the user */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date when the rating was given */,
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the reviews table, to link ratings with reviews */,
  "spoiler" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the rating contains a spoiler */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_review_id FOREIGN KEY ("review_id") REFERENCES reviews ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the platform */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the user */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the user */,
  "preferences" TEXT /* JSON array of preferred genres */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie reviewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who provided the review */,
  "review_text" TEXT /* Text of the user's review */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the review was written */,
  "spoiler" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the review contains a spoiler */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the ratings table, to link reviews with ratings */,
  "helpful_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the review was marked as helpful by other users */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_rating FOREIGN KEY ("rating") REFERENCES ratings ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "box_office" (
  "box_office_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each box office record */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated by the movie */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when the movie was released */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the movie was released */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget of the movie in dollars */,
  "opening_weekend" REAL /* Revenue generated during the opening weekend */,
  "distributor" TEXT /* Distributor of the movie */,
  "screen_count" INTEGER /* Number of screens the movie was shown on */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("box_office_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_box_office_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_cast" (
  "cast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cast entry */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the actor */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Name of the character played by the actor */,
  "order" INTEGER /* Order of the actor in the cast list */,
  "character_gender" TEXT /* Gender of the character played by the actor */,
  "character_age" INTEGER /* Age of the character played by the actor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_cast_actor_id FOREIGN KEY ("actor_id") REFERENCES actors ("actor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_cast_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actors" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each actor */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Name of the actor */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Birth date of the actor */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the actor */,
  "height" INTEGER /* Height of the actor in centimeters */,
  "weight" INTEGER /* Weight of the actor in kilograms */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Brief biography of the actor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_crew" (
  "crew_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crew entry */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "crew_member_id" INTEGER /* ID of the crew member */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the crew member in the movie */,
  "order" INTEGER /* Order of the crew member in the crew list */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role played by the crew member */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crew_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_crew_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_crew_crew_member_id FOREIGN KEY ("crew_member_id") REFERENCES crew_members ("crew_member_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crew_members" (
  "crew_member_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crew member */,
  "crew_member_name" TEXT /* Name of the crew member */,
  "role_type" TEXT /* Type of role the crew member typically has */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the crew member */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Brief biography of the crew member */,
  "awards" TEXT /* JSON array of awards won by the crew member */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crew_member_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_keywords" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each keyword */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* Keyword associated with the movie */,
  "importance" TEXT /* Importance of the keyword (e.g., 'high', 'medium', 'low') */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_keywords_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "keywords" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each keyword */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* Keyword associated with movies */,
  "synonyms" TEXT /* JSON array of synonyms for the keyword */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id")
)
